title: U.S. Is Negotiating an Iran Deal That Would Buy Time, Again
summary: The United States proposed a 20-year “suspension” of all nuclear activity, even as President Trump demands assurances that Iran can never build a nuclear weapon.

title: Amid War, G.O.P. Puts Off Testimony From Senior Pentagon Commanders
summary: House lawmakers had been scheduled to hear testimony next week from the head of military operations in the Middle East, but the Republican majority postponed the hearing until late May.

title: What the U.S. Blockade of Iran and the Strait of Hormuz Might Look Like
summary: The U.S. military has provided few details on how it might carry out President Trump’s orders as he seeks to pressure Tehran on a peace deal. But history and established practices offer some clues.

title: Eric Swalwell Says He Will Resign From Congress After Sex Abuse Accusations
summary: The California lawmaker said in a social media post Monday that he would quit. He has denied accounts of abusing women.

title: Tony Gonzales, a Texas Congressman, Says He Will Resign
summary: The third-term Republican lawmaker is accused of coercing a staff member into a sexual relationship.

title: What Happens in California Governor’s Race After Eric Swalwell Stepped Down?
summary: For Democrats, the drama is reverberating well beyond the state.

title: Trump’s Explanation for an Image of Himself as Jesus: ‘I Thought It Was Me as a Doctor’
summary: The image showed President Trump bathed in divine light and clad in religious robes. His interpretation was that the image depicted him as a doctor, not Jesus Christ.

title: With Algeria Visit, Pope Leo Returns to His Augustinian Roots
summary: Some theologians say the pontiff’s understanding of Augustine’s teachings helped inform his response to President Trump’s negative comments about him.

title: Vance Says Pope Leo Should Stay Out of U.S. Affairs
summary: The vice president, a Catholic, defended President Trump, who had accused the pope of being too liberal and “weak on crime.” The pontiff has said that he has “no fear of the Trump administration.”

title: Canada Special Elections 2026 Results: Carney’s Liberal Party Gains Majority
summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney, constrained by leading a minority government for the past year, gained a majority in the House of Commons after special elections on Monday.

title: He Warned About the Dangers of A.I. If Only His Father Had Listened.
summary: Ben Riley was already writing about the risks of chatbots when his dad started trusting A.I. over his doctor.

title: Trump’s Erratic Behavior and Extreme Comments Revive Mental Health Debate
summary: As the president threatens to wipe out Iran and attacks the pope, even some former allies and advisers are questioning whether he has grown increasingly unbalanced, describing him as “lunatic” and “clearly insane.”

title: Greg Abbott Threatens to Cut $110 Million From Houston Police Over ICE Policy Dispute
summary: The office of Gov. Greg Abbott wants the city to stop enforcing a new ordinance governing how its officers engage with ICE.

title: Trump and MAGA Embraced Orban. Now, They’re Worried by His Loss in Hungary’s Election.
summary: Viktor Orban, the Hungarian prime minister, and his right-wing playbook were embraced by parts of the American right. Now some are worried by his defeat.

title: ‘We Finally Have Democracy’: Hungarians Erupt in Joy and Relief
summary: Many Hungarians, exhausted by a struggling economy and worried about a move away from the European Union, were jubilant after Prime Minister Viktor Orban conceded. Some had wondered if he would.

title: New Rules Hinder Foreign Firms From Moving Supply Chains From China
summary: Multinationals in China are concerned that the regulations could allow authorities to penalize companies and executives for shifting supply chains away from the country.

title: Sid Krofft, 96, Dies; Created Zany TV Shows for Children of All Ages
summary: With his brother Marty, he released a string of wildly inventive programs, including “H.R. Pufnstuf” and “Land of the Lost.” Some became cult favorites, even among adults.

title: A Famed Brooklyn Cemetery Wants You to Come Visit … Before You Die
summary: Green-Wood Cemetery in Brooklyn is opening a $43 million visitor’s center to attract the living by making it easier to navigate the rambling grounds.

title: I Went to China to See Their Progress on A.I. We Can’t Beat Them.
summary: American leaders should be trying to cooperate with China on A.I.

title: Is It Too Much to Ask to Care About Sudan?
summary: I have seen what can happen when Americans care about a wrong in the world.

title: Late Night Takes Sides in the President vs. the Pope
summary: Jimmy Kimmel said President Trump’s social media post aimed at Pope Leo XIV “is what happens when you sell Bibles instead of reading them.”

title: Oasis, Phil Collins and Sade to Join Rock & Roll Hall of Fame
summary: Billy Idol, Luther Vandross and Wu-Tang Clan will also be inducted, while New Edition, Mariah Carey and Melissa Etheridge failed to make the final cut.

title: About 90 People Detained at N.Y.C. Protest Over Arms Sales to Israel
summary: The protesters sat in the middle of Third Avenue in Midtown Manhattan and blocked traffic for about an hour.

title: Netflix Leader Makes Rare Overture to Cinema Owners
summary: Ted Sarandos attended a major movie theater conference for the first time and met with domestic and international owners, people familiar with the meetings said.
